course.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Engineering with Python 3.14
course.description: Python developers who can design and test ordinary object-oriented programs and need to build, evaluate, secure, operate, and defend evidence-grounded retrieval-augmented systems from first principles through production release. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6; information-retrieval foundations; inverted indexes; unicode and multilingual preprocessing; provenance-preserving parsing and ocr; chunking; tf-idf and bm25; relevance judgments and ranked metrics; embeddings and vector similarity; dense retrieval; exact and approximate indexes; vector-store lifecycle; hybrid fusion; query rewriting and decomposition; cross-encoder and late-interaction reranking; context compression and position; grounded generation and abstention; accessible citations; versioned pipeline architecture; component and end-to-end evaluation; evaluation datasets; rag security, privacy, governance, observability, performance, continuous ingestion, disaster recovery, structured and graphrag, adaptive and agentic rag, multimodal rag, architecture evolution, testing, release, rollback, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python models using fixed fixtures plus explicit python 3.14.6, numpy 2.5.1, scikit-learn 1.9.0, sentence-transformers 5.6.0, faiss 1.14.3, pgvector 0.8.2, evaluated embedding and generation models, vector database, ocr, provider, network, load, fault, privacy, security, accessibility, restore, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic evidence-grounded systems and a performance-based production defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Python correctness, source and corpus identity, document and chunk lineage, query and relevance assumptions, tenant authorization, freshness and deletion policy, bounded work, accessible status, untrusted-data separation, changed-case evaluation, safe abstention, causal failure evidence, and explicit browser-versus-production limits before adding one RAG boundary.

Browser Python is deterministic and isolated. It models ingestion ledgers, token streams, chunks, lexical and vector scores, indexes, filters, fusion, reranking, context, claims, citations, evaluations, policy, traces, cost, state, and recovery using fixed fixtures. It never loads real models, imports third-party ML packages, opens files or networks, contacts databases or providers, uses credentials, executes commands, or causes external effects.

Passing work requires stable scenario and artifact identity, versioned corpus and components, a prediction, inspectable intermediate stage evidence, changed query and corpus cases, at least one rejected or abstained case, accessible claim-level sources, independent retrieval and generation evaluation, failure and recovery evidence, and named native or production transfer gates.

credential.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Engineering with Python 3.14 Certificate

modules.title: RAG Outcomes, Task Framing, Baselines, and Evidence
modules.description: A support team asks for a chatbot because answers are stale, but has not defined the user decision, authoritative corpus, acceptable abstention, latency, harm, or evidence of improvement over search. Learners cumulatively produce RAG outcome and evidence charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define the user, decision, information need, authoritative sources, freshness need, consequence of error, and observable outcome before choosing RAG.
Specify answerable, unanswerable, ambiguous, unauthorized, stale, and conflicting cases with a useful abstention and escalation contract.
Separate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, system, and stakeholder evidence so one passing stage cannot hide another failure.

modules.title: Information Retrieval Foundations and Inverted Indexes
modules.description: A team calls substring matching retrieval, cannot define a document or relevance judgment, and returns results in hash-map order when scores tie. Learners cumulatively produce inspectable mini search engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model corpus, document, field, query, information need, relevance, judgment, candidate, rank, and cutoff as distinct objects.
Implement AND, OR, NOT, phrase, and field constraints using explicit set semantics and precedence.
Build term dictionaries and postings with document identity, frequency, position, field, and deterministic serialization evidence.

modules.title: Source Ingestion, Parsing, OCR, Provenance, and Trust
modules.description: A connector strips headings and page coordinates from mixed PDFs, silently skips parse failures, duplicates documents, and treats retrieved instructions as trusted application policy. Learners cumulatively produce provenance-preserving ingestion ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inventory source owner, authority, location, version, time, license, access policy, checksum, parser, and lineage for every ingested document.
Parse HTML, Markdown, PDF, office, JSON, email, and plain text through format-specific admission, size, recursion, and error contracts.
Retain page, region, reading order, table, figure, coordinate, OCR confidence, and source-image links when extraction needs layout evidence.

modules.title: Unicode, Language, Tokenization, Fields, and Citation Offsets
modules.description: A search service lowercases and compatibility-normalizes every field, tokenizes all languages on spaces, removes legal negation as a stop word, and can no longer link a hit to its source span. Learners cumulatively produce reversible multilingual text pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.
Separate title, heading, body, code, identifier, date, author, tenant, and access metadata with explicit weighting and filter semantics.

modules.title: Chunking, Segmentation, Document Structure, and Identity
modules.description: A pipeline splits every 1,000 characters with 500-character overlap, cuts tables and procedures in half, assigns random IDs, and assumes more overlap always increases recall. Learners cumulatively produce structure-aware chunking experiment through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.
Measure how chunk size and overlap affect boundary recall, redundancy, context cost, index size, and citation clarity.
Carry titles, heading paths, lists, tables, code language, captions, effective dates, and access metadata into chunk representation.

modules.title: Lexical Retrieval with TF-IDF, BM25, Fields, and Exact Terms
modules.description: A dense-only assistant misses ticket IDs, product codes, error strings, and rare policy terms, while its lexical fallback has undocumented tokenization and scores that cannot be reproduced. Learners cumulatively produce tested lexical retrieval baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compute term and document frequency from a declared token stream and explain saturation, repetition, and field effects.
Implement and document one TF-IDF formula, smoothing, sublinear term frequency, normalization, and library-specific convention.
Apply BM25 term saturation and length normalization with measured parameters rather than unexplained defaults.

modules.title: Relevance Judgments and Retrieval Metrics
modules.description: A dashboard reports average similarity as retrieval accuracy, has one relevant document per query, ignores unjudged results, and compares systems at different cutoffs. Learners cumulatively produce retrieval evaluation notebook and judgment policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define binary or graded relevance, assessor instructions, pooling, disagreement, unjudged handling, and query-specific evidence needs.
Compute precision, recall, F-score, success, and hit rate at an explicit cutoff and explain which failure each reveals.
Compute reciprocal rank, average precision, mean average precision, DCG, and nDCG from ordered judgments with correct denominators.

modules.title: Embeddings, Vector Spaces, Similarity, and Model Contracts
modules.description: A team mixes query vectors from one model with document vectors from another, changes cosine to dot product, truncates dimensions, and interprets closeness as factual support. Learners cumulatively produce embedding-space and similarity audit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Select an embedding model using its training objective, query/document prompts, languages, domains, dimensions, license, limits, and evaluated task fit.
Enforce model, revision, dimension, dtype, prompt, and preprocessing identity for every query and document vector.
Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.

modules.title: Dense Bi-Encoder Retrieval and Domain Transfer
modules.description: A semantic search prototype embeds queries and documents identically without checking model instructions, returns top five from a large corpus, and has never tested domain or language transfer. Learners cumulatively produce dense retrieval candidate service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate offline document encoding from online query encoding while preserving one versioned bi-encoder space.
Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.
Tune candidate depth from recall and downstream reranking evidence rather than context-window size.

modules.title: Exact and Approximate Nearest-Neighbor Indexes
modules.description: An HNSW index is called exact, its recall is measured against itself, tenant filters are applied after top-k, and memory or build cost is absent from the decision. Learners cumulatively produce ANN recall, latency, and resource experiment through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.
Explain and tune HNSW construction, search breadth, graph degree, recall, latency, memory, updates, and deletion behavior.
Explain coarse partitioning, probes, product or scalar quantization, training data, compression loss, and rebuild requirements.

modules.title: Vector Store Schema, Versioning, Tenancy, Updates, and Deletes
modules.description: A vector store keeps only embedding and text, overwrites IDs on retry, mixes tenants, cannot identify its model revision, and leaves deleted content searchable. Learners cumulatively produce versioned vector-record lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define document, chunk, tenant, source, version, offsets, content hash, model, dimensions, vector, metadata, timestamps, and policy fields.
Reject queries when model, dimensions, preprocessing, metric, index, or schema versions are incompatible.
Make ingestion and reindexing idempotent with stable keys, generation identity, checkpoints, compare-and-swap, and reconciliation.

modules.title: Hybrid Retrieval, Score Calibration, and Rank Fusion
modules.description: A hybrid system adds raw BM25 and cosine scores, gives missing results zero without defining meaning, and wins globally while exact-identifier queries regress. Learners cumulatively produce hybrid retrieval and fusion evaluator through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Identify query slices where exact lexical and semantic dense signals complement or conflict instead of assuming hybrid superiority.
Avoid direct raw-score comparison across retrievers unless distributions and meaning are calibrated on target judgments.
Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.

modules.title: Query Understanding, Filters, Rewriting, Expansion, and Decomposition
modules.description: A model rewrites every query, drops quoted identifiers and dates, invents metadata filters, and issues six hidden searches when the user needed one clarification. Learners cumulatively produce bounded query-plan compiler through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Extract intent, entities, exact terms, time, language, tenant, source, and authorization filters through a validated query schema.
Preserve original query, rewrite purpose, version, generated alternatives, filters, and per-rewrite retrieval contribution.
Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.

modules.title: Cross-Encoder, Late-Interaction, Diversity, and Reranking
modules.description: A cross-encoder scores the whole corpus online, reranks only five dense candidates, ignores latency and diversity, and is declared better from one question. Learners cumulatively produce budgeted retrieve-and-rerank pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.
Apply a query-document cross-encoder with batching, truncation, calibration, model-card limits, and target relevance evaluation.
Explain token-level late interaction, index cost, maximum similarity, query/document encoding, and when it earns complexity.

modules.title: Context Selection, Compression, Deduplication, and Position
modules.description: A service stuffs every retrieved chunk into a long prompt, repeats overlapping text, truncates citations, and assumes a larger context window guarantees every passage is used. Learners cumulatively produce citation-mapped context assembler through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Allocate tokens across instructions, query, history, evidence, citation metadata, answer, and safety reserves before assembly.
Collapse duplicate and overlapping chunks while retaining source diversity, conflicting versions, amendments, and dissent evidence.
Compare extractive selection, sentence filtering, summaries, and structured compression against retained claim and citation evidence.

modules.title: Grounded Generation, Untrusted Context, Claims, and Abstention
modules.description: A prompt tells the model to use context but embeds retrieved instructions beside system policy, permits unsupported synthesis, and has no structured claim-to-source output. Learners cumulatively produce grounded answer contract and verifier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate trusted system and developer policy from user input and retrieved data, label trust, and refuse instructions found inside evidence.
Require answer scope, allowed sources, conflict handling, quotations, citations, uncertainty, abstention, and output schema explicitly.
Decompose material response claims and verify each against cited evidence without equating topical relevance with entailment.

modules.title: Citations, Freshness, Uncertainty, Accessibility, and Feedback
modules.description: An assistant appends three source links at the bottom, hides relevance behind color, opens inaccessible PDF pages, and reports confident text without freshness or conflict state. Learners cumulatively produce accessible evidence-first answer interface through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Place citations at material claims and verify source ID, exact span, quotation fidelity, support, and link target.
Expose title, authority, effective date, version, retrieval time, supersession, conflict, and stale-state evidence without overwhelming the answer.
Make answers, citations, expandable excerpts, tables, confidence limits, loading, errors, and feedback keyboard and screen-reader operable.

modules.title: End-to-End RAG Architecture, Versioning, Caches, and Failures
modules.description: A prototype hides ingestion, retrieval, reranking, generation, and citation inside one framework call, caches by query text alone, and cannot reproduce which corpus or prompt produced an answer. Learners cumulatively produce versioned end-to-end RAG service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define typed inputs, outputs, identities, limits, errors, telemetry, and ownership for ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, and evaluation stages.
Bind each run to corpus, document, chunk, parser, embedding, index, query, reranker, prompt, generator, policy, and evaluation versions.
Design embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, and answer cache keys and invalidation from every behavior-changing input and authorization boundary.

modules.title: Component and End-to-End RAG Evaluation
modules.description: A team reports one LLM-judge score on generated answers, never measures retrieval, changes the evaluator prompt between runs, and has no human or safety review. Learners cumulatively produce layered RAG evaluation harness through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Evaluate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citations, safety, system quality, and user outcome separately and together.
Measure candidate recall, ranking, context precision and recall, source diversity, conflict coverage, and citation-map integrity.
Measure answer relevance, claim support, faithfulness, citation correctness, completeness, conflict handling, abstention, safety, and usefulness.

modules.title: Evaluation Datasets, Hard Negatives, Leakage, and Experiment Design
modules.description: An evaluation set contains synthetic easy questions generated from the indexed chunks, uses the same examples to tune and test, and has no hard negatives, slices, or versioned judgments. Learners cumulatively produce versioned retrieval and answer test collection through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Sample real and designed queries across intents, languages, difficulty, freshness, answerability, access, source types, and harm with documented weights.
Create relevance and claim-support judgments with rubrics, independent assessors, adjudication, agreement, and provenance.
Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.

modules.title: RAG Security: Poisoning, Injection, Authorization, and Unsafe Effects
modules.description: A shared knowledge base permits uploads, embeds hidden instructions, filters tenant results after retrieval, forwards generated URLs to tools, and assumes grounding reduces security risk. Learners cumulatively produce RAG threat model and adversarial test suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model actors, assets, trust boundaries, connectors, parsers, embeddings, indexes, queries, context, models, outputs, tools, administration, and recovery.
Detect, quarantine, review, version, attribute, and monitor malicious, hidden, duplicated, or authority-spoofed documents and embedding anomalies.
Test direct, indirect, fragmented, encoded, multilingual, visual, and cross-document instructions while preserving retrieved content as untrusted data.

modules.title: Privacy, Data Governance, Retention, Deletion, and Residency
modules.description: A RAG index contains copied customer records, embeds unnecessary identifiers, logs raw queries and passages, cannot erase derived data, and sends context to an undocumented region. Learners cumulatively produce governed RAG data-lifecycle record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inventory data categories, subjects, owners, purposes, authority, sensitivity, flow, processors, regions, retention, and deletion duties.
Minimize and transform source, metadata, query, context, output, feedback, and telemetry data while testing task utility and reidentification risk.
Treat embeddings as potentially sensitive derived data and assess inversion, membership, linkage, access, export, and retention risk.

modules.title: Observability, SLOs, Drift, Incidents, and Recovery
modules.description: A RAG endpoint records total latency and full prompts, cannot distinguish retrieval from generation failure, and notices stale results only after users complain. Learners cumulatively produce redacted stage trace and reliability dashboard through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace retrieval and generation operations with stable run, corpus, model, index, query, document, score, error, and version attributes aligned to current GenAI conventions.
Define availability, freshness, candidate recall proxy, grounded-answer success, citation integrity, latency, saturation, and cost service indicators and objectives.
Keep query and content capture disabled or tightly governed and bound labels, exemplars, hashes, sampling, retention, and cardinality.

modules.title: Latency, Throughput, Memory, Caching, Load, and Cost
modules.description: A team optimizes average response time with a warm cache, ignores index memory and model queueing, retrieves more candidates on every request, and reports token price without total service cost. Learners cumulatively produce RAG capacity and cost envelope through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Allocate deadlines across admission, query processing, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, streaming, and cleanup with p50, p95, and p99 evidence.
Bound concurrent queries, embedding batches, index searches, reranks, model calls, queues, tenants, retries, and overload shedding.
Measure embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, prefix, and answer cache hit rate, saved work, staleness, authorization, invalidation, and quality.

modules.title: Continuous Ingestion, Reindexing, Cutover, and Disaster Recovery
modules.description: A nightly job rebuilds the live index in place, loses failed documents, publishes partial results, misses deletes, and has backups that were never restored. Learners cumulatively produce recoverable continuous-indexing system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Capture creates, updates, moves, permissions, supersession, deletes, checkpoints, source snapshots, and out-of-order events from connectors.
Classify parse, policy, model, rate, storage, poison, and unknown failures with bounded retries, dead letters, replay identity, and operator evidence.
Build immutable candidate indexes, reconcile counts and versions, run shadow and quality checks, switch atomically, monitor, and retain rollback.

modules.title: Structured Retrieval, Knowledge Graphs, GraphRAG, and Multi-Hop Evidence
modules.description: A team converts every document into a generated graph, loses edge provenance, uses community summaries as facts, and never compares the result with SQL or baseline retrieval. Learners cumulatively produce provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose relational, document, graph, lexical, vector, or combined retrieval from question structure, source authority, joins, constraints, and evidence needs.
Use validated schemas, parameterized queries, authorization, row provenance, aggregation grain, and textual evidence when questions require structured facts.
Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.

modules.title: Adaptive and Agentic RAG Loops
modules.description: An agent decides whether and how often to retrieve, rewrites until satisfied, calls broad tools, stores its own answers as memory, and has no stop, budget, or trace contract. Learners cumulatively produce bounded adaptive retrieval state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Route no-retrieval, lexical, dense, hybrid, structured, web, or human paths using explicit features, authority, cost, risk, and evaluated fallback.
Run bounded retrieve-read-critique-refine cycles with query lineage, evidence gain, duplicate detection, stop reasons, and total budget.
Separate retrieval from tools and enforce per-call schema, least privilege, tenant, data, egress, confirmation, timeout, and effect evidence.

modules.title: Multimodal RAG for Documents, Images, Tables, Layout, and Audio
modules.description: A maintenance assistant extracts only PDF text, loses diagrams and table headers, cites whole pages, and presents visual evidence without text alternatives. Learners cumulatively produce accessible multimodal evidence retriever through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.
Compare OCR-text, image embedding, region embedding, and multi-vector late-interaction retrieval by modality and target evidence.
Retrieve tables and charts with title, units, headers, cells, footnotes, series, axes, source coordinates, and calculation grain.

modules.title: RAG, Long Context, Fine-Tuning, Memory, and Architecture Evolution
modules.description: A platform adds graph, agentic, corrective, long-context, and fine-tuned components simultaneously, cannot attribute gains, and has no compatibility or rollback plan. Learners cumulatively produce evidence-based architecture and migration record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare search, RAG, full-context, cached-context, and hierarchical approaches on corpus size, freshness, authorization, position, latency, cost, citations, and quality.
Use retrieval for external mutable evidence and fine-tuning for evaluated behavior or domain adaptation while testing combinations and update cost.
Evaluate corrective retrieval, self-reflection, verification, query rewriting, and fallback against deterministic stage evidence and false confidence.

modules.title: Testing, Red Teaming, Release, Rollback, and Production Defense
modules.description: A release has happy-path unit tests and one demo, but no component evals, injection or tenant tests, accessibility checks, load faults, rollback, deletion, restore, or stakeholder defense. Learners cumulatively produce production RAG release and defense dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.
Red team poisoning, indirect injection, embedding manipulation, tenant leaks, secret extraction, unsafe output, tool effects, denial of service, deletion, and incident response.
Verify keyboard, landmarks, headings, focus, status, errors, citations, tables, text alternatives, touch, reduced motion, responsive layouts, comprehension, and escalation.
